expectations.text: Output identifies the GROUP BY temp table or filesort as a key bottleneck
expectations.evidence: Problems Identified table: P1 'Using temporary; Using filesort for GROUP BY across columns from two tables... Creates temp table on disk, major I/O cost'

expectations.text: Output proposes a structural rewrite (derived table with pre-aggregation, subquery-to-join, or join reordering)
expectations.evidence: Strategy 3: pre-aggregates orders inside a derived table (SELECT o_custkey, o_orderpriority, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders ... GROUP BY o_custkey, o_orderpriority) then joins to customer

expectations.text: Output includes at least one index recommendation with CREATE INDEX syntax
expectations.evidence: Index Recommendation section: CREATE INDEX idx_customer_mktsegment_custkey ON customer(c_mktsegment, c_custkey)

expectations.text: Plan comparison is included with before/after metrics on rows examined or cost estimates
expectations.evidence: Key Metric Changes table: rows, cost, temp table, filesort, join input compared before/after (~65% cost reduction)

expectations.text: Output explains why the rewrite reduces intermediate result size or eliminates temp table operations
expectations.evidence: Why this works: 'Moves aggregation before the join, radically reducing the join input size... eliminates the post-join GROUP BY (which was the primary temp-table bottleneck)'
